title: Odontites odontites
taxonomy: Odontites odontites is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. The plant is native to Europe and Western Asia. Odontites odontites is often found in dry, open areas such as grasslands and scrublands. It is a perennial herb that typically grows to be around 10-30 centimeters tall.

intro: Odontites odontites, also known as downy toothwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, which is characterized by its unique, tooth-like leaves and white or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with its roots and leaves believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Odontites odontites include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
